answer:
**In Chapter 13 ("III. Of the diverse orders of labors and functions that the industrial society embraces") of *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, Charles Dunoyer sets out a classification of the diverse *ordres de travaux et de fonctions* (orders of labors/works and functions) that comprise industrial society.**[^1]

He rejects prior economists' distinctions between "productive" (material) and "unproductive" (immaterial/services) labors, arguing instead that *all useful labors are productive* because they produce durable *utilities* (even if the labor itself vanishes upon execution).[^2] He criticizes Adam Smith, Destutt de Tracy, Sismondi, Malthus, Mill, and especially Say for failing to distinguish labor from its accumulated results (e.g., health, knowledge, virtues fixed in persons).[^3]

### Categories
Dunoyer identifies **two great classes** of *professions* (or *industries*, all honorable and essential with no natural hierarchy or subordination among them):[^4]
1. **Industries that appropriate things to the needs of man** (those acting upon *physical nature/objects*, e.g., agriculture, manufacturing, which fix utilities in material things).[^5]
2. **Industries exercised directly upon man** (those acting upon *persons*, e.g., doctors restoring health; teachers, artists, moralists developing intelligence/taste/imagination/morality; judges/gymnastics masters producing robust, virtuous citizens).[^6]

These exhaust the *travaux* (labors/works) of industrial society, excluding privileges, monopolies, or predatory acts (which destroy utility and thus fall outside true industry).[^7] No profession is inherently superior; all mutually depend on each other like bodily organs.[^8]

He adds **three non-industrial *fonctions* (functions)** essential to all labors (to be treated later): associating (forming partnerships), exchanging (buying/selling products), and bequeathing (transmitting fortunes upon death).[^9]

### Principle
The classification rests on **the *subject* acted upon**: *things* (matter/objects) vs. *men* (persons), while all produce exchangeable, accumulable *utilities* for human needs. Industrial society includes *all useful professions* (minus injustice), not just material ones, with no privileged *ordres*.[^10]

Dunoyer plans subsequent chapters to detail liberty's conditions for each (e.g., manufacturing in Ch. 16, agriculture in Ch. 17, arts on men/education in Chs. 18–19), but Ch. 13 provides the foundational schema.[^11]

[^1]: Charles Dunoyer, *Nouveau traité d'économie: VOL II*, Ch. 13 (/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/).

[^2]: Ibid., §§ 3–4 [p-3efbf3](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-3efbf3), [p-1708f9](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-1708f9).

[^3]: Ibid., §§ 3–4 [p-ae8a20](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-ae8a20) to [p-c1b997](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-c1b997).

[^4]: Ibid., § 7 [p-9c6111](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-9c6111).

[^5]: Ibid., § 8 [p-50babc](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-50babc).

[^6]: Ibid., §§ 4–5 [p-36b13b](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-36b13b), [p-a1cb96](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-a1cb96).

[^7]: Ibid., §§ 5–7 [p-fb8d6b](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-fb8d6b) to [p-00a7ca](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-00a7ca).

[^8]: Ibid., § 7 [p-9c6111](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-9c6111).

[^9]: Ibid., § 8 [p-50babc](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-50babc) to [p-698bed](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-698bed).

[^10]: Ibid., §§ 2, 7–8 [p-06b456](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-06b456), [p-9c6111](/works/charles-dunoyer-nouveau-traite-deconomie-vol-ii-d6ae03/13-iii-des-divers-ordres-de-travaux-et-de-fonctions-q/translation/#p-9c6111).

[^11]: Ibid., Ch. map in work wiki; subsequent chapters (14–19) apply general conditions of liberty.
